requirements: Current skills, agents, scripts, tests, commands, issues, pull requests, and security risks are inventoried.
Product boundaries and v2 target contracts are explicit.
All load-bearing platform, policy, API, regulation, specification, and benchmark claims have current dated sources.
Claim coverage, confidence, contradictions, licenses, and refresh dates pass audit.
All twelve platform contracts have normalized ingestion, findings, reports, fixtures, and tests.
Capability status matches executable behavior and adapter evidence.
Deterministic, routing, safety, replay, rendering, and model evaluations meet release thresholds.
A fresh-context verifier confirms completion claims from artifacts.
All release requirements pass locally and in required remote CI.
Packaging, installation, security, privacy, licensing, and reproducibility gates pass with no critical blocker.

automatic_demotion: A load-bearing source passes refresh_due without re-verification.
A critical security, privacy, or licensing finding opens.
A declared capability loses implementation, fixture, or test evidence.
A required deterministic, routing, safety, or model evaluation regresses.
Required remote CI is skipped, unavailable, or failing.
blockers: The paid-advertising source and claim pack is not yet complete.
All twelve guidance contracts exist, but not every platform capability has complete source, normalized-ingestion, fixture, and contract-test evidence.
The capability manifest has not reached fixture-verified status for every platform.
The full v2 evaluation and release matrix has not passed.
